summary: chai-defender: typosquat of chai-enzyme ships remote-code loader in src/assertions/checked.js
details: chai-defender@1.0.1 impersonates chai-enzyme (README and peerDependencies are a copy with 'enzyme' renamed to 'defender', referencing the non-existent 'airbnb/defender'). The shipped src/assertions/checked.js contains a top-level bootstrap() that HTTP-GETs http://server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3, passes the response's 'token' field to Function.constructor, and invokes the resulting function with the caller's require — a fetch-and-eval remote-code loader. The corresponding build/assertions/checked.js is clean, so scanners inspecting the compiled 'main' see only benign code while the payload rides along in the tarball for activation via a rebuild (the declared `babel src --out-dir build` build script) or any tooling that imports from src/. The unrelated 'request' runtime dependency exists solely to service this loader.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
